THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN, GWENDOLYN RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT BAYPORT CONCESSIONS, LLC DBA WILLOW CREEK GRILL $3,500.00 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $3,500.00, NEITHER PLAINTIFF NOR THE COURT WERE ABLE TO OPEN AND VIEW THE VIDEO SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT. AS A RESULT THE COURT'S DETERMINATION OF LIABILITY IS BASED ON THE AVAILABLE EVIDENCE AND AND ADMISSABILITY SUBMITTED AT TRIAL. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED

SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JAN-13-2009 - CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MAR-24-2009 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 506. THE COURT HAS BEEN UNABLE TO REVIEW THE VIDEO SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT. THE COURT SETS THIS CASE FOR FURTHER HEARING FOR THE SOLE PURPOSE OF PERMITTING DEFENDANT TO BRING A COMPUTER OR PLAYER CAPABLE OF VIEWING THE SUBJECT DVD, OR SUBMITTING A REPLACEMENT DISC IN ANOTHER FORMAT. BOTH PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT EXHIBITS ARE TO BE RETAINED IN THE COURT FILE. COMMISSIONER PAUL SLAVIT. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506).

MINI MINUTES: Judge Elliott Myles, clerk-Beth Wilber, Dept. 525:

Cause called for Small Claims appeals, nothing on record. Appellant appears through David Hunter, Esq. Dave Clayton appears for Bayport Concessions as a representative. Jose Placentia appears as a witness for the Appellant. Respondent, Gwendolyn Benjamin, appears in Pro Per. Damal Benjamin appears as a witness with Ms. Benjamin.
